column1: # The wheel scrolls the viewport without moving the cell pointer —
# modern convention. Several wheel-down ticks reveal lower rows; the
# pointer stays at A1 (and may scroll off-screen). Wheel-up reverses
# and saturates at row 0. Scroll never changes the mode.
# Wheel down three times — body should now show rows starting well
# below row 1. Default body shows ~20 rows, so 3 ticks * 3 rows/tick
# is enough that row 1 has scrolled out of the viewport but the
# Wheel up twice — viewport rolls back toward the top.
# Many more wheel-ups — must saturate at row 0, not underflow.
